rulings.Case Title: ANNA HERNANDEZ ET AL VS. OLD NAVY, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Approval Of The Parties Paga Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Thursday, May-08-2025, LINE 1. PLAINTIFF ANNA HERNANDEZ', INDIVIDUALLY AND AS AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES PURSUANT TO THE PRIVATE ATTORNEYS GENERAL ACT, ("PAGA"), AND ON BEHALF OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA AND OTHER AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES Motion For Court Approval Of The Parties Paga Settlement. The unopposed motion for approval of the PAGA settlement is granted. The court will endorse the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL VS. VALKEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Thursday, May-08-2025, LINE 2. PLAINTIFF KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. Health and Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (f)(4) provides: "If there is a settlement of an action brought by a person in the public interest under subdivision (d), the plaintiff shall submit the settlement, other than a voluntary dismissal in which no consideration is received from the defendant, to the court for approval upon noticed motion, and the court may approve the settlement only if the court makes all of the following findings: (A) The warning that is required by the settlement complies with this chapter. (B) The award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. (C) The penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in paragraph (2) of subdivision (b)." The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for endorsement. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ABDUL RASHID PATEL ET AL VS. NASIR PATEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Post-Judgment Assignment Order (C.C.P. 708.510)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Thursday, May-08-2025, LINE 3. 1-PLAINTIFFS ABDUL PATEL, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE ABDUL RASHID PATEL AND ZARIN ABDUL RASHID PATEL AB LIVING TRUST, and ZARIN PATEL'S Motion For Post-Judgment Assignment Order (C.C.P. 708.510). Plaintiff's motion for post-judgment assignment order is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff obtained a judgment against defendant Nasir Patel. (Claassen Decl., Ex. A.) Plaintiff seeks to satisfy that judgment by obtaining rents from 1040 Folsom St. because defendant has an ownership in that property. (Claassen Decl., Exs. C, D.) Abulala, LLC is the master tenant of 1040 Folsom St. and it is unclear what rent, if any, it pays to defendant. (N. Patel Decl., pars. 4-7.) The LLC is an entity separate from defendant and plaintiff has not made a sufficient showing in his moving papers to disregard the legal separation. (Curci Investments, LLC v. Baldwin (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 214, 220-221.) Plaintiff's opening papers do not demonstrate a basis for reverse piercing of the LLC's veil and plaintiff's attempt to rehabilitate the motion by supplying evidence in support of reverse piercing in the reply is improper. (Valentine v. Plum Healthcare Group, LLC (2019) 37 Cal.App.5th 1076, 1089 ["New evidence is generally not permitted with reply papers."]; Regency Outdoor Advertising, Inc. v. Carolina Lanes, Inc. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 1323, 1333 [improper to raise new arguments on reply].) No party has made a motion to seal. All parties are required to redact financial account numbers (except the last four digits where necessary) from publicly filed papers. (See Rule of Court 1.201(a).) Plaintiff is ordered to present for public filing substitute versions of all papers containing financial account numbers by May 22, 2025, and to supply service copies to defendant. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL THOMAS VS. SEAMUS COLLINS CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have The Truth Of Matters Requested For Admission To Seamus Collins Construction Inc Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Thursday, May-08-2025, LINE 8. 2-PLAINTIFF MICHAEL THOMAS' Motion To Have The Truth Of Matters Requested For Admission To Seamus Collins Construction Inc Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions. Plaintiff Michael Thomas's unopposed motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in his Requests for Admission, Set One, served on Seamus Collins Construction Inc. ("SCCI") is granted. SCCI failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted by SCCI. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) Thomas also seeks sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $910 payable by SCCI to Thomas. SCCI shall remit sanctions within 20 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
